mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show negative mean performance during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 compared to additive models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations during early-cycle phases, or the autocorrelation-adjusted t-statistic falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 2.8% threshold forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65% of high-inflation periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for term premium movements.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting higher corporate revenue forecasts and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which directly supports near-term equity price appreciation and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal fails to predict 5-day return direction with accuracy exceeding 52%, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing supply capacity, which reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 60% of contraction periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 48% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
